title: [EPIC]: Pantry-Aware AI Recipes — Turn current stock into reviewable meal drafts
label: epic
summary: Add a lightweight AI assistance layer that turns the user’s current pantry into recipe drafts they can review, save, and plan from. The feature should improve meal discovery without adding chat overhead or weakening trust in the current pantry-first workflow.
problem_hl: The app already helps users track stock and rank existing recipes, but it cannot synthesize new meal ideas from the pantry they actually have. That leaves a gap between knowing what is in stock and deciding what to cook, especially when the user has ingredients on hand but no matching recipe in their saved library.
goals: Generate recipe drafts from the full pantry context without requiring a separate chat workflow
Keep AI output reviewable and trustworthy by validating structure before it reaches the UI
Reuse the existing recipe model and persistence flow rather than introducing a parallel AI-only recipe store
Show clearly which ingredients are already in stock versus still missing
Fail safely when the AI provider is unavailable or returns unusable output
scope: Phase 1: Build pantry context assembly and OpenAI-backed structured draft generation on the server
Phase 2: Add a draft review experience with explicit save and save-plus-plan actions
Phase 3: Expose the feature in the main app surfaces and add evaluation and fallback coverage
out_of_scope: Automatic shopping list mutation from AI output
Automatic pantry deduction or meal-plan insertion without user confirmation
acceptance: A signed-in user can request pantry-aware recipe drafts from the app using their current pantry and settings as context
AI responses are validated against a strict structured schema before being rendered or saved
Each draft shows which ingredients can come from the pantry and which ingredients are still missing
No AI-generated recipe is persisted until the user explicitly approves it
An approved AI draft can be saved into the existing recipes and recipe_ingredients tables without schema changes
Provider failures and malformed model output surface a clear fallback message instead of breaking the plan or recipe flows
child_issues: P2-ai-03 AI recipe draft review surface
